Paxalisib.ref: Wen et al., paxalisib in newly diagnosed GBM, unmethylated MGMT (Phase 2)
Paxalisib.note: Brain-penetrant PI3K/mTOR inhibitor; the pivotal GBM program behind Regimen B/C anchor.

Metformin.ref: Sato et al., Glioma-initiating cell elimination by metformin (FOXO3/AMPK)
Metformin.note: Metformin depletes glioma stem cells via AMPK-FOXO3.

Metformin.ref: Metformin inhibits growth of human GBM cells & enhances therapy
Metformin.note: Supports metformin as a chemo/RT sensitizer in GBM.

Chloroquine/HCQ.ref: Phase I/II hydroxychloroquine + RT/TMZ in newly diagnosed GBM
Chloroquine/HCQ.note: Autophagy inhibition; defined tolerable HCQ dose with chemoradiation (dose-limiting toxicity noted).

Atorvastatin.ref: Anticancer effects of mevalonate-pathway modulation in GBM (in vitro/in vivo)

Celecoxib.ref: CUSP9* protocol for recurrent GBM (celecoxib is a component)
Celecoxib.note: Celecoxib as one of 9 repurposed drugs in the multi-target CUSP9 design.

Mebendazole.ref: Antiparasitic mebendazole shows survival benefit in 2 preclinical GBM models
Mebendazole.note: Foundational GBM efficacy signal that launched clinical interest (JHU).

Valproic acid.ref: Postradiation sensitization by HDAC inhibitor valproic acid
Valproic acid.note: HDAC-i radiosensitization; clinical GBM data later mixed — interpret cautiously.

Adavosertib(WEE1).ref: WEE1 inhibitor adavosertib with radiation in diffuse glioma (trial)
Adavosertib(WEE1).note: WEE1 abrogation + RT in glioma; the p53-null synthetic-lethal handle in action.

ATR inhibitor.ref: DNA damage response inhibitors (ATR-CHK1-WEE1) for glioblastoma — review
ATR inhibitor.note: Frames ATR inhibition against replication stress from p53 loss + telomere stress.

6-thio-dG.note: Rationale for exploiting TERT+ status; 6-thio-dG is experimental, not pharmacy-available.

CUSP9 protocol.ref: Kast et al., conceptual multi-drug 'Coordinated Undermining of Survival Paths'
CUSP9 protocol.note: The original framework for the exact all-weaknesses-at-once strategy you're pursuing.

CUSP9 protocol.ref: Efficacy of coordinated pharmacological blockade in GBM stem cells
CUSP9 protocol.note: Preclinical support that the combined blockade hits GBM stem cells.

_removed_disulfiram.reason: Removed from active drug library after DIRECT randomized trial (negative).

evidence.ref: DIRECT: Disulfiram+Copper + chemo vs chemo alone in recurrent GBM (RANDOMIZED)
evidence.note: KEY NEGATIVE RESULT: DIRECT randomized trial (n=88, recurrent GBM) — disulfiram+Cu added to chemotherapy did NOT significantly improve 6-month survival vs chemotherapy alone, and caused significantly more grade >=3 adverse events (34% vs 11%) and serious adverse events (41% vs 16%). Authors concluded it should NOT be recommended for recurrent GBM. This substantially lowers disulfiram's evidence grade.

evidence.ref: Skrott et al. mechanism — disulfiram/Cu targets NPL4-p97 segregase
evidence.note: Molecular basis: Cu-DDC aggregates NPL4 -> proteotoxic stress.
